mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer spending and aggregate demand, which drives corporate earnings growth and lifts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following month regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between RSAFS level and 20-day forward returns is ≤ 0 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 55% of periods with above-median RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.5%, it signals labor market weakness that erodes consumer income and spending, reduces corporate profit expectations, and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward return in periods when UNRATE > 4.5 is not negative with t-test p < 0.10, or the signal produces positive returns in more than 40% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The combined signal of accelerating GDP growth (GDPC1 quarterly increase > 0.5%) and elevated short-term rates (FEDFUNDS > 5%) reflects overheating that forces the Fed to tighten further, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term (GDPC1 growth * FEDFUNDS) on 60-day forward returns remains above -0.03 over any 3-year window, or more than 45% of triggered instances produce positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: During an industrial production recovery phase (INDPRO rising above 103 and continuing to increase month-over-month), the supply-side expansion boosts productivity and corporate output, lifting earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods when INDPRO > 103 and month-over-month change is positive, the average 20-day forward return is not positive with statistical significance (t-test p > 0.05), or less than 55% of such periods have positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 1,500 thousand units signals strong demand in the housing sector, which stimulates construction activity, consumer spending on durables, and broader economic momentum, pushing S&P 500 returns higher over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return following HOUST > 1500 is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or the hit rate of positive returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment (PAYEMS) exceeds 160,000 thousand and the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is below 4.0%, the labor market is extremely tight, fueling wage growth and inflationary pressures that force the Fed to tighten, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint condition produces negative average 20-day forward returns with less than 60% probability, or the interaction effect (measured by cross-sectional rank regression) has a coefficient greater than -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: The consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) in an always-on trend signal: as inflation rises, it erodes real corporate earnings and forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, both of which are negative for equity prices, causing the S&P 500 to decline over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between CPIAUCSL level and 60-day forward returns is consistently non-negative over any 5-year rolling window, or the proportion of negative returns in high-CPI periods is not significantly above 50%.

mechanism_narrative: During a demand-led recovery phase (real personal consumption expenditures rising strongly, proxied by RSAFS), robust consumer spending drives corporate revenue and earnings growth, which lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: In periods classified as demand-led recovery (RSAFS above its trailing 12-month average and GDPC1 QoQ growth > 0.3%), the average 5-day forward return is not positive with t-test p < 0.05, or the hit rate of positive returns is below 55%.
